input_row.title: Ensure that Activity Log Alert exists for Delete Policy Assignment
input_row.description: Create an activity log alert for the Delete Policy Assignment event.
input_row.rationale: Monitoring for delete policy assignment events gives insight into changes done in "azure policy - assignments" and can reduce the time it takes to detect unsolicited changes.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Navigate to the Monitor blade. 2. Click on Alerts. 3. In the Alerts window, click on Alert rules. 4. Ensure an alert rule exists where the Condition column contains Operation name=Microsoft.Authorization/policyAssignments/delete. 5. Click on the Alert Name associated with the previous step. 6. Ensure the Condition panel displays the text Whenever the Activity Log has an event with Category='Administrative', Operation name='Delete policy assignment' and does not filter on Level, Status or Caller. 7. Ensure the Actions panel displays an Action group is assigned to notify the appropriate personnel in your organization. Audit from Azure CLI az monitor activity-log alert list --subscription <subscription ID> --query "[].{Name:name,Enabled:enabled,Condition:condition.allOf,Actions:actions}" Look for Microsoft.Authorization/policyAssignments/delete in the output Audit from PowerShell Get-AzActivityLogAlert -SubscriptionId <subscription ID>|where-object {$_.ConditionAllOf.Equal -match "Microsoft.Authorization/policyAssignments/delete"}|select-object Location,Name,Enabled,ResourceGroupName,ConditionAllOf Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: c5447c04-a4d7-4ba8-a263-c9ee321a6858 - Name: 'An activity log alert should exist for specific Policy operations'
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Navigate to the Monitor blade. 2. Select Alerts. 3. Select Create. 4. Select Alert rule. 5. Choose a subscription. 6. Select Apply. 7. Select the Condition tab. 8. Click See all signals. 9. Select Delete policy assignment (Policy assignment). 10. Click Apply. 11. Select the Actions tab. 12. Click Select action groups to select an existing action group, or Create action group to create a new action group. 13. Follow the prompts to choose or create an action group. 14. Select the Details tab. 15. Select a Resource group, provide an Alert rule name and an optional Alert rule description. 16. Click Review + create. 17. Click Create. Remediate from Azure CLI az monitor activity-log alert create --resource-group "<resource group name>" --condition category=Administrative and operationName=Microsoft.Authorization/policyAssignments/delete and level=<verbose | information | warning | error | critical> --scope "/subscriptions/<subscription ID>" --name "<activity log rule name>" -- subscription <subscription id> --action-group <action group ID>  Remediate from PowerShell Create the conditions object $conditions = @() $conditions += New-AzActivityLogAlertAlertRuleAnyOfOrLeafConditionObject - Equal Administrative -Field category $conditions += New-AzActivityLogAlertAlertRuleAnyOfOrLeafConditionObject - Equal Microsoft.Authorization/policyAssignments/delete -Field operationName $conditions += New-AzActivityLogAlertAlertRuleAnyOfOrLeafConditionObject - Equal Verbose -Field level Retrieve the Action Group information and store in a variable, then create the Action object. $actionGroup = Get-AzActionGroup -ResourceGroupName <resource group name> - Name <action group name> $actionObject = New-AzActivityLogAlertActionGroupObject -Id $actionGroup.Id Create the Scope variable. $scope = "/subscriptions/<subscription id>" Create the Activity Log Alert Rule for Microsoft.Authorization/policyAssignments/delete. New-AzActivityLogAlert -Name "<activity log alert rule name>" - ResourceGroupName "<resource group name>" -Condition $conditions -Scope $scope -Location global -Action $actionObject -Subscription <subscription ID> -Enabled $true Default Value: By default, no monitoring alerts are created. References: 1. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-in/azure/azure-monitor/platform/alerts-activity-log 2. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-in/rest/api/monitor/activitylogalerts/createorupdate 3. https://docs.microsoft.com/en- in/rest/api/monitor/activitylogalerts/listbysubscriptionid 4. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/mcsb-logging-threat- detection#lt-3-enable-logging-for-security-investigation 5. https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/services/blueprints/ Additional Information: This log alert also applies for Azure Blueprints.
input_row.reference: 1. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-in/azure/azure-monitor/platform/alerts-activity-log 2. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-in/rest/api/monitor/activitylogalerts/createorupdate 3. https://docs.microsoft.com/en- in/rest/api/monitor/activitylogalerts/listbysubscriptionid 4. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/mcsb-logging-threat- detection#lt-3-enable-logging-for-security-investigation 5. https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/services/blueprints/ Additional Information: This log alert also applies for Azure Blueprints.

first_opinion.response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control can be fully verified via Azure CLI and PowerShell commands, which are explicitly mentioned in the audit section.
- Azure Resource Manager APIs are available to query the activity log alerts for the Delete Policy Assignment event.

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_monitor_activity_log_alerts_delete_policy_assigned_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
The automated program would check the following:

- Use the Azure CLI command to list all activity log alerts:
  ```
  az monitor activity-log alert list --subscription <subscription ID> --query "[].{Name:name, Enabled:enabled, Condition:condition.allOf, Actions:actions}"
  ```

- Alternatively, use PowerShell to retrieve activity log alerts:
  ```
  Get-AzActivityLogAlert -SubscriptionId <subscription ID>
  ```

- Specific resources to enumerate include:
  - Activity Log Alerts associated with policy assignments under the specified subscription.

- The program would validate the following configuration properties/settings:
  - Check if an alert exists where the condition includes:
    - `Operation name=Microsoft.Authorization/policyAssignments/delete`
    - Event category `Administrative`
  
- Clear Pass/Fail criteria:
  - **Pass:** An alert rule exists with the specified operation name condition and has an action group assigned.
  - **Fail:** No alert rule exists or the condition does not meet the specified criteria.

- Example API endpoint for retrieving alerts:
  ```
  GET https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/<subscription_id>/providers/microsoft.insights/activitylogalerts?api-version=2021-04-01
  ```

By evaluating these aspects, the program can automatically determine compliance with the control to ensure that the necessary activity log alerts are in place for Delete Policy Assignments.

second_opinion.response:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. The control can be effectively verified using Azure CLI, PowerShell, and Azure Resource Manager (ARM) APIs. These tools provide the necessary capabilities to automate the verification of activity log alerts for the Delete Policy Assignment event.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 9

- The automation details are technically accurate. The Azure CLI command and PowerShell cmdlet are correctly specified for listing activity log alerts. The API endpoint provided is also correct for retrieving alerts.
- The program name follows a logical convention: `azure_monitor_activity_log_alerts_delete_policy_assigned_enabled`.
- The criteria for Pass/Fail are well-defined and align with the control requirements.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **Error Handling and Logging:** While the automation details are solid, the assessment could benefit from specifying error handling and logging mechanisms to ensure that any issues during execution are captured and addressed.
- **Subscription Scope:** Ensure that the automation script accounts for scenarios where multiple subscriptions are managed. This could involve iterating over a list of subscriptions if applicable.
- **Action Group Verification:** While the assessment mentions verifying if an action group is assigned, it could be more explicit about the types of actions expected (e.g., email notifications, webhook triggers) to ensure comprehensive alerting.
- **Security Considerations:** Ensure that the automation script adheres to security best practices, such as secure handling of credentials and permissions required to execute the commands.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The assessment is well-grounded in Azure's capabilities and accurately describes the process for automating the verification of the control. The use of Azure CLI, PowerShell, and ARM APIs is appropriate and effective for this purpose.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** Keep as-is

- The assessment is technically sound and appropriately classified as Automated. The suggested improvements are enhancements rather than corrections, and they do not detract from the validity of the current approach.

final_decision.response:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:** Both AI opinions agree that the control can be effectively verified using automated methods such as Azure CLI, PowerShell, and Azure Resource Manager APIs. These tools provide the necessary capabilities to check the existence of activity log alerts for the Delete Policy Assignment event, making automation feasible and efficient.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:** 
azure_monitor_activity_log_alerts_delete_policy_assignment_exists

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **Azure Resource/Service:** Azure Monitor
- **API/CLI Method:** 
  - Azure CLI: `az monitor activity-log alert list --subscription <subscription ID> --query "[].{Name:name, Enabled:enabled, Condition:condition.allOf, Actions:actions}"`
  - PowerShell: `Get-AzActivityLogAlert -SubscriptionId <subscription ID>`
- **Properties to Check:** 
  - Ensure an alert rule exists where the Condition column contains `Operation name=Microsoft.Authorization/policyAssignments/delete`.
- **Pass Criteria:** 
  - An alert rule is present with the specified condition for the Delete Policy Assignment event.
- **Fail Criteria:** 
  - No alert rule exists with the specified condition, indicating non-compliance.

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:** There were no disagreements between the AI opinions; both supported the automated approach.
